Morocco Is Emerging as a Renewable Energy Superpower
headlines.summary: Morocco is rapidly becoming a renewable energy powerhouse thanks to its favourable weather conditions and proximity to Europe. The North African country has rapidly developed its solar energy sector and is now looking to become a major green hydrogen and sustainable shipping hub.

headlines.title: Kansas Fed Pres Warns Oil Price Shock Might Not Be Transitory
headlines.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City President Jeffrey Schmid has warned that the current global energy shock cannot simply be dismissed as transitory, given already-elevated baseline inflation. headlines.title: Secretive Azerbaijan Deal Raises Energy Security Concerns in Georgia
headlines.summary: Recently signed deals between Georgia and Azerbaijan are raising fresh questions about Tbilisi’s strategic direction and energy security. headlines.title: Oil Prices Suffer Biggest Weekly Collapse in Two Months
headlines.summary: Brent crude is heading for its biggest weekly drop in two months as markets bet on a 60-day ceasefire extension and partial reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: Philippines Receives First Iranian Crude Cargo Since Hormuz Blockade
headlines.summary: The Philippines has received its first cargo of Iranian crude, Reuters reported on Friday, citing tanker-tracking data from Kpler and Vortexa. headlines.title: India's Central Bank Warns Oil Shock Threatens Growth

headlines.summary: Just about two months ago, JPMorgan did the math on "How Long Before The World Hits Crude Oil Operational Minimum." headlines.title: Japan Crude Imports Fell 66% in April
headlines.summary: Amid the supply disruption in the Middle East, Japan’s crude oil imports crashed by 66% in April from the same month last year, official Japanese data showed on Friday.
